title: TENET
logline: A secret agent must master the physics of time inversion to prevent a Russian oligarch from triggering a temporal apocalypse orchestrated by the future.
antagonistOrConflict: Andrei Sator, a dying Russian oligarch communicating with the future to assemble an Algorithm that will invert the entropy of the world
tone: Cerebral, propulsive, enigmatic—combines Bond-style action with hard sci-fi concepts; maintains emotional restraint while exploring temporal paradox

actBreaks.description: After the Oslo Freeport heist, the Protagonist wakes having encountered inverted objects and a masked figure, fundamentally shifting from espionage thriller into temporal warfare.

validation.ruling: Scene #108 fundamentally shifts the story's direction as the Protagonist reveals the existence of temporal war to Neil, decides to trust him despite the risk, and commits to returning to Mumbai for answers—this irreversible decision transforms the mission from covert investigation into active recruitment and escalation, with the Protagonist now building a team and pursuing Sator directly.

actBreaks.description: At the Oslo War Memorial, the Protagonist learns he must prevent Sator's suicide on his yacht in Vietnam while simultaneously raiding Stalsk-12 in a temporal pincer movement—the mission structure crystallizes.

validation.ruling: Scene #193 delivers the crucial revelation that transforms the entire mission: Priya explains the algorithm was created by a future scientist who split it into nine pieces to prevent world inversion, that Sator is collecting these pieces, and that the Protagonist has been manipulated into this suicide mission—this exposition fundamentally reframes the stakes from arms dealing to preventing temporal apocalypse and launches the final act's race to stop Sator.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: Nolan constructs a palindromic narrative that mirrors its own physics. The first act (scenes 1-108) establishes conventional espionage—Kiev opera siege, recruitment, Mumbai intelligence gathering, Oslo heist—before the Freeport encounter ruptures linear causality. Act Two (109-193) operates as investigation and preparation: the Protagonist learns inversion mechanics, recruits Kat as leverage against Sator, and discovers the Algorithm's threat through the Tallinn highway sequence (scenes 135-175), which itself contains forward and backward action simultaneously. The final act (194-275) executes a temporal pincer at Stalsk-12 while Kat confronts Sator on his yacht in Vietnam—two timelines converging on the same moment from opposite directions. The structure is less three-act than it is a Möbius strip: the ending contains its own beginning, with Neil revealed as potentially inverted from the future throughout. Nolan withholds full comprehension until late, trusting momentum over clarity.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The script maintains relentless forward drive despite its temporal complexity. The opening Kiev sequence (scenes 1-17) establishes breakneck action rhythm. A deceleration occurs during exposition scenes with Barbara (scenes 30-32) and Priya (scenes 109-110), but these are brief. The Tallinn highway heist (scenes 135-175) is the structural centerpiece—40 scenes of sustained, spatially intricate action where forward and inverted timelines collide. The final Stalsk-12 operation (scenes 194-271) intercuts between three temporal streams and two locations across 78 scenes, creating a fractal editing pattern. Scene length averages 1.1 pages, but key action sequences fragment into rapid cuts (scenes 143-167 average 0.3 pages each). The script trusts visual storytelling over dialogue in its second half.

incitingIncident.description: The Protagonist wakes on a boat and Fay reveals he's been in a medically induced coma while they extracted him from Ukraine and rebuilt his mouth; the Protagonist realizes the suicide pills were fake sedatives as a test, learns his team didn't get clear, and chooses to die rather than give up his colleagues.

validation.ruling: Scene #19 shatters the Protagonist's status quo by revealing the torture and suicide pill were a test, his team is dead, and he's been declared dead himself—forcing him into a new reality where he must operate outside his former organization with only a cryptic word ('Tenet') as his entry point into a mysterious cold war.

midpoint.description: The Protagonist is pushed into a chair in a red-lit chamber divided by glass, where on the blue-lit other side Sator enters backwards pulling Kat in a respirator, shoves her into a chair, and places his gun against her head while playing recordings in reverse to interrogate the Protagonist, who eventually reveals the algorithm is in the BMW's glove box after Sator threatens to shoot Kat.

validation.ruling: Scene #165 is the point of no return where the Protagonist witnesses Sator interrogating him through inverted time with Kat held at gunpoint and a bullet already embedded in the glass between them—this revelation of temporal warfare's true mechanics and Kat's shooting raises stakes to life-and-death urgency and shifts the Protagonist from reactive investigation to proactive mission to save her and stop Sator.

climax.description: The truck drags them away as the ground explodes and collapses, the line snaps, and Ives pulls the Protagonist back onto solid ground as the subsidence approaches.

validation.ruling: Scene #264 shows discovery of the algorithm but is not the peak confrontation. Scene #267 is the true climax where the Protagonist and Ives are dragged away clutching the algorithm as the ground explodes and collapses beneath them, the line snaps, and they barely escape the subsidence—this is the highest tension moment (rated 10) where they physically secure the algorithm while surviving the hypocentre's destruction, resolving the main story question of whether they can prevent world annihilation.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. VAN, PLAZA, DOWNTOWN KIEV, UKRAINE – DAY
scenes.summary: A driver wakes four black-clad Americans in a van as police flood a plaza in Kiev; the Protagonist cocks his weapon with eyes closed, then the team shoulders weapons and pulls on helmets as a Ukrainian SWAT van arrives.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: SWATs mass at each entrance of a concert hall lobby while the Protagonist watches gas canisters being brought to the air-conditioning system, and the SWATs pull on gas masks.

scenes.heading: INT. BOX, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A well-dressed man in a box watches people slump unconscious in a wave below, then turns to see a uniformed official motion calm and draw a sidearm.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATRE, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A terrorist wets a rag and ties it around his face while others don respirators and face masks, then they cock their weapons in preparation.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The SWATs breach and pour into the theatre, exchanging gunfire with the terrorists.

scenes.heading: INT. CURVING CORRIDOR – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist runs silently through curving corridors, splits from his team at a junction, and checks each box alone.

scenes.heading: INT. BOX, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist bursts into a box, takes out an official, exchanges code phrases with the well-dressed man to confirm his identity, warns him he's been made and has two minutes to decide, then asks where the bombs are.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The battle between SWATs and terrorists rages in the corridor as SWATs move down checking boxes one by one.

scenes.heading: INT. BOX, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist smashes glass and they drop on a rope into the stalls where the well-dressed man feigns sleep among unconscious audience; terrorists open fire hitting sleeping audience members, the Protagonist holds the man's ankle to keep him still, then jumps up and returns fire.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist races across the lobby under gunfire, leaps into the coat-check area, finds a bomb counting down, locates a sports bag with a black metallic object inside using a coat-check ticket, stuffs it into his pack and scrambles over the counter.

scenes.heading: INT. SERVICE CORRIDOR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist runs through a service corridor checking door numbers, stops at one, throws it open and jumps in low.

scenes.heading: INT. UTILITY ROOM – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist finds three SWATs with guns trained and the well-dressed man inside; he throws a bomb to one SWAT, shows the object to the well-dressed man who confirms it's real, then orders the man to swap clothes with a SWAT and instructs them to take the object and use the man's exit through service tunnels.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATRE, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and the disguised target race through sleeping audience collecting bombs as timers tick down; a SWAT puts a gun to the Protagonist's head creating a standoff while the target aims at the SWAT, then the Protagonist notices smoke puffing backward into a bullet hole in a chair seat.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAZA, DOWNTOWN KIEV – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and target emerge from the concert hall as an explosion occurs above them.

scenes.heading: INT. THEATRE, CONCERT HALL – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Innocent civilians stir under the explosion in the boxes.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAZA, DOWNTOWN KIEV – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The van pulls up with rear door open and they jump inside.

scenes.heading: INT. VAN – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist pulls off his mask and breathes; the passenger turns and grabs the target's face sensing something wrong, then the Protagonist turns to find the driver pointing a gun at him before the screen goes black and the Protagonist's screaming is heard.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist is betrayed and captured, shifting from successful extraction to torture and interrogation.

scenes.heading: EXT. RAIL YARDS – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist is tied to a chair in a rail yard while the driver with bloody pliers explains a man can hold out eighteen hours, gestures at a clock showing colleagues will clear by 7, pushes over the tortured target who didn't last eighteen minutes, then pulls out a knife and leans toward the Protagonist.

scenes.heading: INT. BOAT AT SEA – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist wakes on a boat and Fay reveals he's been in a medically induced coma while they extracted him from Ukraine and rebuilt his mouth; the Protagonist realizes the suicide pills were fake sedatives as a test, learns his team didn't get clear, and chooses to die rather than give up his colleagues.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist learns he passed a loyalty test by choosing death, establishing his character and introducing the larger mission beyond national interests.

scenes.heading: EXT. DECK OF THE BOAT – LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist stands on deck contemplating the horizon, tells Fay he resigns, but Fay reveals he's dead and no longer works for them; Fay explains his duty transcends national interests for everyone's survival in a cold war where knowing its nature means losing, then gives him only a word and a gesture as his way in.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist is recruited into a mysterious organization fighting for global survival, establishing the central conflict and his new mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAUNCH, WIND FARM AT SEA – DUSK
scenes.summary: The Protagonist is driven on a launch toward giant wind turbines at sea, climbs a ladder to a door on one turbine as the launch departs, then enters.

scenes.heading: INT. WIND TURBINE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist finds a sports bag containing passports, cash, credit cards, a hi-viz vest, and three silver suicide pills which he tosses back into the bag while shaking his head.

scenes.heading: INT. WIND TURBINE – LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist eats, drinks, and exercises by climbing the endless interior ladder of the turbine as his food and water supply shrinks, doing pull-ups at the very top with feet dangling over the vertiginous drop.

scenes.heading: INT. WIND TURBINE – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Protagonist is woken by a repeated air horn and opens the door.

scenes.heading: EXT. WIND TURBINE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist finds a large catamaran with maintenance crew in hi-viz vests servicing the turbine, pulls on his vest and climbs down onto the catamaran.

scenes.heading: EXT. EUROPEAN PORT – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist disembarks with the wind-farm crew; a driver in hi-viz steps out of line leaving a car door open with engine running and GPS set, and the Protagonist gets in.

scenes.heading: EXT. OFFICE COMPLEX – LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist arrives at a bland office park wearing hi-viz and carrying a clipboard, heading inside past numerous staff coming out.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, OFFICE COMPLEX – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist moves through the lobby unimpeded and checks tenant listings to find 'Windfarm Transitions – B-2'.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist walks up to B-2 where a woman named Barbara tells him a hi-viz vest and clipboard can get you almost anywhere, then he interlaces his fingers and recites an obscure tenet before she ushers him through a door with a key card.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE, LABORATORY – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Barbara hands the Protagonist tea and explains he's here for 'how' not 'what', reveals they're trying to prevent World War Three, and when he asks about nuclear holocaust she says something worse.

scenes.heading: INT. SHOOTING RANGE – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Barbara hands the Protagonist an empty semi-automatic; he fires it and a shot rings out, then discovers a round now in the magazine; she places two identical bullets on a table and one flies up into her hand falling in reverse, demonstrating inverted ammunition that travels backward through time and explaining inverted weapons could affect the past.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist witnesses inverted technology for the first time, establishing the core science fiction concept and the nature of the threat.

scenes.heading: INT. ARCHIVE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Barbara shows the Protagonist an archive of drawers containing rusted inverted material including screws, nails and casings, explaining they're finding more inverted objects now that they know what to look for.

scenes.heading: EXT. CROWDED STREET, MUMBAI – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist walks out of an electronics store in Mumbai opening a new phone, dials and exchanges code phrases, requests an assist to get to Sanjay Singh, and is told Singh never leaves his house while the Protagonist looks up at Singh's high-rise.

scenes.heading: INT. BOMBAY YACHT CLUB – EVENING
scenes.summary: The Protagonist meets a businessman named Neil at the Bombay Yacht Club who says getting an audience with Sanjay Singh isn't possible and getting out alive is the problem; Neil asks if he'd take a child or woman hostage, keeps thinking, orders drinks including a diet Coke for the Protagonist without asking, and reveals he knows the Protagonist never drinks on the job.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH-RISE HOUSE – EVENING
scenes.summary: Armed guards patrol the balconies of a high-rise house.

scenes.heading: INT. HIGH-RISE HOUSE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sanjay Singh mixes drinks inside one of the magnificent rooms.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOW ROOFTOP NEXT DOOR TO HIGH-RISE HOUSE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil and the Protagonist race across a rooftop carrying a sports bag, set up a winch and catapult, and the Protagonist fires a line up and over the balcony railing of the high-rise house.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGH-RISE HOUSE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil launch themselves on elastic lines, fly up to land against the building, run silently up to the upper terraces and shoot two guards with silenced pistols.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. HIGH-RISE HOUSE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sanjay Singh hands a drink to a woman in a sari and they move out onto the balcony.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALCONY – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist surprises Sanjay at gunpoint and demands to know who supplied unusual ammunition from Ukraine; Sanjay attempts small talk while the woman pushes a red alarm button, but the Protagonist tells them no one will help.

scenes.heading: INT. SECURITY STATION, HIGH-RISE HOUSE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: An alarm buzzes in the security station but the guards are frozen as Neil holds a gun on them with a finger to his lips.

scenes.heading: EXT. BALCONY, HIGH-RISE HOUSE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist deduces the ammunition's unique metal combination is from India and therefore from Sanjay; Sanjay says guns aren't conducive to negotiation but the Protagonist cocks his weapon, puts it to Sanjay's head, and says he's the man people talk to, then Sanjay tells him to talk to his wife.

scenes.heading: INT. TERRACE OVERLOOKING MUMBAI, SANJAY’S HOUSE – LATER
scenes.summary: Priya Singh reveals she runs the operation and identifies the dealer as Andrei Sator, a Russian oligarch; the Protagonist recites Sator's background and Priya corrects him that Sator made billions from plutonium not gas and feeds British Intelligence disinformation; she explains the rounds were ordinary when she sold them and Sator must have access to a turnstile that inverts objects' entropy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Priya identifies Sator as the antagonist and reveals the existence of turnstiles that can invert entropy, establishing the central threat and mission target.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEMBER’S CLUB, LONDON – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist walks down the busy Mall in London and up the steps of a member's club.

scenes.heading: INT. MEMBER’S CLUB, LONDON – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist enters the member's club and tells a steward he's Mr Crosby's lunch, then follows the steward inside.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE, MEMBER’S CLUB – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist meets Crosby who has started eating without him; Crosby warns that Sator is tapped into intelligence services feeding them rubbish, explains Soviet-era secret cities, reveals Sator grew up in a closed city called Stalsk-12 that's now a ghost town, describes how Sator made a fortune scavenging plutonium during the Soviet collapse, and explains Sator's marriage to Kat is falling apart but he won't let her go because he's a vindictive SOB who thinks she's his possession.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEST LONDON PRIVATE SCHOOL – DAY
scenes.summary: Mothers wait at railings outside a West London private school as schoolkids pile out.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CAR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. SHIPLEY’S, LONDON – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. SHIPLEY’S – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. APPRAISAL ROOM – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist meets Kat Barton in an appraisal room and presents her with a Goya drawing, which she examines with interest.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Protagonist reveals to Kat over dinner that he knows she helped authenticate a fake Goya for her husband Sator, and attempts to leverage this information to meet Sator, but Kat reveals her husband already knows and uses it to blackmail her.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals Sator's control over Kat through blackmail, establishing the emotional stakes and power dynamic that will drive the Protagonist's involvement with both characters.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND RESTAURANT – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Kat exits the restaurant distraught and gets into a waiting Mercedes.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MERCEDES – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kat asks the driver to leave but he watches the rear-view mirror, waiting for something to happen inside the restaurant.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist enters the restaurant kitchen followed by two thugs as chefs and busboys retreat.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. ALLEY BEHIND RESTAURANT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kat pleads to leave but the Gaunt Russian tells her that her husband wants her to watch what happens.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist kicks one thug in the crotch and spins him into another as pots and pans fly.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Volkov eats bread and sauce while enjoying the sounds of the fight from the kitchen.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT KITCHEN – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist dodges a swing, headbutts the third thug in the neck, then grabs him and races toward the window.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MERCEDES – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kat flinches as a body crashes down the stairs, then sees the Protagonist exit unharmed.

scenes.heading: EXT. ALLEY BEHIND RESTAURANT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist brushes himself clean, spots the Mercedes, and starts moving toward it.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MERCEDES – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Gaunt Russian scrambles to drive away as Kat looks back at the Protagonist.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL GATES – DAY
scenes.summary: Kat says goodbye to her son Max at the school gates, hugging him tightly with tears in her eyes before he's driven away, then turns to find the Protagonist waiting behind her; she tells him the drawing is in Oslo at the freeport.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kat agrees to help the Protagonist by revealing the location of the drawing, shifting from resistance to cooperation and setting the Oslo freeport heist in motion.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO OPERA HOUSE – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist briefs Neil on the roof of the Oslo Opera House about the freeport being a transit lounge for valuable items, while intercutting with Kat explaining the system to the Protagonist.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEPORT – DAY
scenes.summary: A staff member gives Neil a tour of the freeport, explaining its Pentagon-based vault structure and biometric security system.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEPORT SHIPPING AREA – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil observes the vast shipping area and notices Rotas containers marked for delivery to Tallinn.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO OPERA HOUSE – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist tells Neil to bring lead-lined gloves and pay attention to fire precautions, revealing they're looking for something nuclear.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEPORT – DAY
scenes.summary: The staff member explains the freeport uses halide gas instead of sprinklers, which displaces all air within seconds and would suffocate anyone in the vaults.

scenes.heading: INT. OSLO AIRPORT – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil explains that during a lockdown the vault doors revert to pickable locks, and the Protagonist questions how they'll get firepower through airport security to trigger a lockdown.

scenes.heading: EXT. CROWDED AIRPORT BUS, OSLO AIRPORT – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil look at the freeport from an airport bus as Neil begins to smile about an idea.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO STREET – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil proposes crashing a transport plane into the freeport's rear wall to start a fire, and introduces Mahir who will work the plane.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM, OSLO – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil hyperventilates and holds his breath for eighty-five seconds while the Protagonist studies plans and notices an unmarked space at the center of the pentagon, then begins hyperventilating himself.

scenes.heading: EXT. FREEPORT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil, carrying briefcases, head inside the freeport at night.

scenes.heading: EXT. APRON, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir and Rohan back a meal truck up to a transport plane and lift cargo to the front door.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil pass through freeport security with briefcases containing framed documents.

scenes.heading: INT. TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir and Rohan carry crew meals into the plane's galley under a guard's supervision.

scenes.heading: INT. GALLERY SPACE/LOUNGE, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The staff member brings Neil an espresso in the gallery lounge as the Protagonist subtly checks his watch.

scenes.heading: INT. TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir and Rohan knock out three guards with chloroform, dump them in the catering truck lift, seal the plane door, and Mahir shows the pilots his gun.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The staff member leads Neil and the Protagonist into the vaults.

scenes.heading: INT. TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The pilots nervously pull the plane back from the hangar at gunpoint while Rohan checks the gold bars in the rear.

scenes.heading: EXT. APRON, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The plane is towed onto the taxiway and begins moving forward.

scenes.heading: INT. TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rohan slashes the restraining straps on the gold bars, stuffs one in his waistband, and pulls the pin on a grenade.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULTS, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The staff member shows Neil and the Protagonist into a vault where Neil puts down his briefcase and pulls out framed documents.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAXIWAY, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The transport plane barrels down the taxiway.

scenes.heading: INT. TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rohan tosses the grenade behind the gold and ducks as it explodes.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAXIWAY, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The explosion blasts a hole in the plane's rear and gold bars pour out across the tarmac.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULTS, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Hearing the distant explosion, the Protagonist and Neil start hyperventilating while the confused staff member watches.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir forces the pilots from their seats.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAXIWAY, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The inflatable slide bursts open and the pilots scramble down and roll away from the plane.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir turns the stick hard left, aiming the plane at the rear wall of the freeport.

scenes.heading: INT. TRANSPORT PLANE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rohan and Mahir climb down a ladder behind the forward landing gear.

scenes.heading: EXT. TAXIWAY, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir and Rohan jump onto the asphalt and race away as the plane smashes into the freeport wall and erupts in a massive explosion.
scenes.turningPointNote: The plane crash triggers the lockdown and fire that enables the heist, marking the transition from preparation to active infiltration of the vault system.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULTS, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The vault is rocked by the explosion, alarms scream, doors start closing, the staff member bolts, halide gas hisses in, and the Protagonist and Neil break apart their document frames into lockpicks and pry open the inner door before it seals.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil burst into the corridor gasping for air, then hustle to the next vault door and break it open.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULT 2, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist's lockpick breaks and Neil panics when he finds no handle on the first door, then spots halide gas hissing from a broken roller door; the Protagonist finally opens his door and drags the stumbling Neil through.

scenes.heading: INT. INNER CORRIDOR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil lie gasping in the inner corridor, then Neil hears someone else present; they arrive at the Rotas vault doors and each work on one, with Neil's opening first and then helping the Protagonist open his.

scenes.heading: INT. ROTAS VAULT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist enters a room with a long glass window and bullet holes, finding a stripped automatic pistol on the floor and broken glass, while Neil examines bullet holes in an identical room on the other side of the glass and notices wisps of smoke gathering near them.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of bullet holes, smoke moving strangely, and evidence of a previous confrontation introduces the first direct physical evidence of inverted objects and temporal warfare.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil chases a forwards-moving figure down the corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. ROTAS VAULT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A backwards-moving figure pushes the Protagonist toward a bullet hole while making strange reverse groaning sounds; the Protagonist stabs the figure in the arm with his pick and smacks its gun away, then the figure rises into his arms and pulls him backwards through the door.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist's first physical encounter with an inverted person, experiencing the disorienting reality of reversed causality in combat, fundamentally changing his understanding of the threat.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, VAULT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The backwards figure pushes or pulls the Protagonist down the corridor in an ambiguous motion.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil races around a corner, grabs the masked figure's mask and pulls it off, looks surprised at the face (which we don't see), then turns and races back.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and the backwards figure struggle into another corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULT 2, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and the figure smash into racks and crates; the Protagonist grabs the gun and raises it to the figure's head, but Neil desperately tells him not to kill; the Protagonist throws the figure down, demands to know why they're here and who sent them, steps on the figure's injured arm while trying to rip off the gas mask as reverse screaming grows louder.

scenes.heading: EXT. APRON, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A jet engine has come loose and is still thrusting, swinging around and causing havoc for emergency services including an ambulance that skids sideways; Mahir and Rohan stand at a distance watching the mayhem among dozens of uniformed airport workers.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: EMTs race into the Freeport past the staff member.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULTS, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil works the lock on another door while sirens sound; the Protagonist asks what happened to the other guy and Neil says he took care of him; the door opens and they slip into the outer vault.

scenes.heading: INT. OUTER VAULT, FREEPORT – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil reassemble the document frames, then lie down pretending to be unconscious as emergency services burst in.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM, OSLO – DAWN
scenes.summary: Neil confirms Mahir and Rohan escaped clean, then the Protagonist reveals there's a temporal cold war involving technology that can invert an object's entropy; Neil mentions his physics background and the Protagonist explains they expected to find the drawing but instead found an inverted person, leading Neil to realize the implications are beyond secret.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist reveals the core concept of inversion and the temporal cold war to Neil, establishing the true nature of the conflict.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FERRY BOAT, MUMBAI – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist meets Priya on a ferry and brings up inversion; Priya explains they're trying to divide and contain knowledge of inversion like they couldn't with the atomic bomb, saying ignorance is their ammunition and the more any one person knows the greater the risk of making the situation worse.

scenes.heading: EXT. MUMBAI STREET – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Priya tells the Protagonist to get closer to Sator; the Protagonist says he's been compromised and will likely be killed going back in; Priya says this was always a suicide mission and reveals the Protagonist could offer Sator plutonium 241 that Sator failed to steal at the Kiev opera siege; the Protagonist realizes he was there and asks if they let him in to die, and Priya confirms they knew he'd be okay.
scenes.turningPointNote: Priya reveals the Protagonist was at the Kiev opera siege and provides the key leverage (plutonium 241) to approach Sator, connecting the opening sequence to the larger mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. TERRACE, AMALFI COAST – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist meets Kat on a terrace and tells her she doesn't have to worry about the drawing anymore because he destroyed it; Kat is upset her son spends every day with Sator thinking less of her; the Protagonist asks her to introduce him to Sator and provides her with a detailed cover story about meeting at a party in Riyadh.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOCK, AMALFI – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Kat look at Sator's enormous yacht with its crew, tenders, helicopters, and missile defenses; the Protagonist suggests joining her but Kat notes Volkov isn't taking passengers, so the Protagonist suggests taking his boat instead and they walk past Volkov.

scenes.heading: EXT. HARBOUR, AMALFI – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist guns his boat hard across the harbor with Kat enjoying the speed; he pulls up alongside the yacht where crew members scramble at the unauthorized approach; Kat steps onto the yacht and the Protagonist gives a jaunty salute to a figure above (backlit by sun) who doesn't respond, then zips back toward shore passing Volkov in the yacht's launch.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. YACHT – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Kat enters the yacht's lounge and drops her bags; Sator appears asking who the American is and whether he's the man from Shipley's that Sator tried to have beaten up; Kat confirms they met in Riyadh and she invited him to dinner; Sator tells her Max is visiting Pompeii and Herculaneum.

scenes.heading: INT. RESTAURANT, AMALFI COAST – EVENING
scenes.summary: The Protagonist approaches Sator's table at a restaurant where Volkov frisks him in full view of diners; Sator quietly asks if the Protagonist has slept with his wife yet, then asks how he'd like to die; Sator reaches a greasy palm to the Protagonist's cheek and threatens to take him to a walled garden to torture and kill him unless he tells the truth.

scenes.heading: EXT. DINING ROOM, YACHT – MORNING
scenes.summary: Kat tells Sator she's taking Max back to England because he's missed too much school, but Sator refuses and says Max has everything he needs on the yacht; Kat accuses him of not caring what Max needs and says he bought the trappings of a king but they both know he's a peasant; a servant places a covered plate in front of her.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOCK, AMALFI – MORNING
scenes.summary: The Protagonist stands on the dock dressed for sailing and watches the launch approach; he greets them but Kat ignores him while Sator enjoys this.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPEN OCEAN, AMALFI – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist tries to catch Kat's eye but she won't look at him; Sator hands him a harness and helmet and the Protagonist looks ahead to see two high-tech America's Cup sailing yachts.

scenes.heading: EXT. SAILING YACHT DECK – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Kat, Sator, and the Protagonist board the sailing yacht; the rigging crew departs; Sator asks if the Protagonist knows how to use the harness and gestures that Kat is captain.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPEN OCEAN, AMALFI – LATER
scenes.summary: The boats fly across the water on hydrofoils with Sator and the Protagonist harnessed on one pontoon and Kat at the wheel; Sator asks about the Kiev opera siege and the Protagonist explains the missing plutonium 241 surfaced there; Kat swings the boat and they duck under the boom, racing to the other pontoon; Sator's harness comes loose and he falls into the water.

scenes.heading: INT. STATEROOM, SATOR’S YACHT – DAY
scenes.summary: Kat bursts into the Protagonist's stateroom and smashes her fists into his chest, demanding why he didn't let Sator drown; the Protagonist says he needs Sator and reveals he's not who she thinks; Kat says Sator showed her the drawing and the Protagonist apologizes; Kat asks what he's done and the Protagonist says Sator is much more than an arms dealer but he can't tell her what.

scenes.heading: INT. STUDY, SATOR’S YACHT – EVENING
scenes.summary: The Protagonist enters Sator's study where a doctor is taking Sator's blood pressure; Sator dismisses the doctor and pours vodka, saying he owes the Protagonist his life; the Protagonist asks for no retribution against Kat as payment; Sator laughs and says it was his own mistake, then agrees to help steal the plutonium 241 in exchange for the Protagonist's help getting the drawing back from the forger.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sator agrees to help the Protagonist steal the plutonium in exchange for retrieving the forged drawing, establishing their alliance and the next phase of the plan.

scenes.heading: INT. KAT’S STATEROOM, SATOR’S YACHT – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kat hides a gun under her mattress, feels the lump, moves it to her jewelry case on the desk, then jumps onto the bed with a book when she hears a noise; Sator enters and locks the door, removes a diamond cufflink and his belt, slips the cufflink through a belt hole creating a diamond-studded weapon, and asks if she wants to be quiet while testing it against his palm.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATOR’S YACHT, AMALFI COAST – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sator grabs binoculars from one of his men and peers out as a helicopter emerges from the darkness, thundering in.

scenes.heading: INT. GUEST STATEROOM, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist listens to the incoming chopper, pulls on a black windbreaker, and quietly opens his door.

scenes.heading: INT. PASSAGE, BELOW DECKS, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist makes his way through the passage below decks, looking for an exit.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATOR’S YACHT, AMALFI COAST – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The helicopter descends to the landing pad with rotors turning as Sator signals his men to head inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist watches from a roof overlooking the helipad as Sator's men remove a large, grubby capsule and take it below while the chopper lifts off.

scenes.heading: INT. STORAGE AREA, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist makes his way through the storage area and peers through a window.

scenes.heading: INT. ENGINE ROOM, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sator opens the capsule and lets small flat gold bars leap from it into his hand; the crew shoves a crew member forward onto his knees who pulls a gold bar from his rear pocket and offers it to Sator with trembling hands; Sator takes it and smashes the crew member in the mouth and over the head with the gold bar.

scenes.heading: INT. STORAGE AREA, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist is repulsed by what he sees through the window.

scenes.heading: INT. ENGINE ROOM, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sator steps back breathless from the motionless crew member on the ground and checks his fitness tracker as blood drips from the gold bar in his hand, noting his pulse is ninety-eight.

scenes.heading: EXT. STORAGE AREA, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist senses someone, turns, and Volkov smashes his jaw, kicks him in the ribs, and pistol-whips him.

scenes.heading: INT. ENGINE ROOM, SATOR’S YACHT – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The bleeding Protagonist is shoved into the engine room; Sator demands to know who he is and how he knows about the opera; the Protagonist claims to work for several intelligence services and says the CIA provides two-thirds of the market for fissile material; Sator threatens him with a bullet and mentions Tallinn; the Protagonist spots a radiation symbol on the capsule.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist spots the radiation symbol on the capsule, confirming the inverted material connection, and Sator mentions Tallinn, setting up the next major operation.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOWNTOWN STREETS, TALLINN – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist walks with Neil in Tallinn discussing the gold, which has no identifying marks; the Protagonist theorizes Sator uses dead drops by burying time capsules and digging them up to collect inverted material; Neil reports the soil sample shows northern Europe or Asia with radiation and European dried blood; they discuss the material arriving Thursday and not knowing when Sator arrives.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. MERCEDES ON TALLINN STREETS – DAY
scenes.summary: Kat sits beside Sator in the back of a Mercedes; Sator tells her everything salvaged from Oslo has been shipped to Tallinn; Kat asks why she's there and Sator says he doesn't trust anyone else to assess the pieces.

scenes.heading: EXT. FREEPORT, DOCKSIDE AREA, EDGE OF TALLINN – DAY
scenes.summary: The Mercedes pulls into the Tallinn Freeport with bodyguards emerging; Sator heads inside and Kat follows, looking through a metal fence at an idling Audi whose driver is wearing an oxygen mask.

scenes.heading: INT. STORAGE FACILITY, TALLINN FREEPORT – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Kat enters the storage facility where Sator stands at a table covered in weapons; he picks up a machine gun and asks if it's salvageable; Kat says it's not her area of expertise; Sator says this is where their worlds collide and accuses her of trying to negotiate around the filthy business that pays for their lifestyle; Volkov reports the convoy is due downtown in ten minutes; Sator tells Kat it's time to go but she refuses, so he slams the machine gun down and tells Volkov to bring her.

scenes.heading: EXT. TALLINN STREET – DAY
scenes.summary: A large unmarked truck with an escort front and back makes its way through downtown traffic.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BMW, TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Neil watch the convoy cross an intersection in their BMW, then pull forward to get ahead; Neil raises a walkie-talkie and the yellow co-pilot radios that green has two minutes.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. GREEN TRUCK, TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The green driver nods at the green co-pilot who confirms on the radio, then pulls out a large automatic rifle.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BMW, TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FIRE ENGINE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BMW, TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. STORAGE FACILITY – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. MERCEDES – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. STORAGE FACILITY, TALLINN FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. TALLINN STREETS – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FIRE TRUCK – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SECURITY SUV – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The security driver tries the radio but gets only static.

scenes.heading: INT. CONTROL CENTRE, TALLINN POLICE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A police operator reports the radio is down to his supervisor, who confirms the convoy is still moving on screen.

scenes.heading: EXT. TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The convoy grinds forward as co-pilots blast dye packs onto the SUV windshields, blinding the guards, while the Protagonist climbs onto the ladder and swings out over the nuclear truck, fixing a frame to its roof.

scenes.heading: INT. NUCLEAR TRANSPORTATION TRUCK – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist lands on the nuclear truck, gets his bearings, finds the safe, and reaches into his pack.

scenes.heading: EXT. TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A police car pulls up behind the fire truck, the policemen spot sparks from the dragging SUV bumper, and the passenger tries his radio but gets only static.

scenes.heading: INT. NUCLEAR TRANSPORTATION TRUCK – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist affixes a charge to the safe, runs a wire, pushes back into the corner, and blasts the door open.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FIRE ENGINE – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Red Passenger watches the police through his mirror, rolls down his window, and props his assault rifle on the sill.

scenes.heading: INT. NUCLEAR TRANSPORTATION TRUCK – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist reaches into the safe, pulls out an orange plastic case, and reaches up to climb through the roof.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FIRE TRUCK – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist climbs onto the ladder with the orange case, Red Passenger shoots out the police car's engine block, the ladder swings the Protagonist back to the fire truck, and he jumps into the BMW which speeds away.
scenes.turningPointNote: The heist succeeds — the Protagonist escapes with the orange case containing what appears to be the target object.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BMW, TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil confirms they got it, but the Protagonist opens the case to reveal a black metal shape the size of a softball that he doesn't recognize as any weapons class encapsulation, and Neil insists it's what Sator is after while the Protagonist asks him to check the radio chatter.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BACKWARDS AUDI – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The backwards Audi continues to accelerate with no one at the wheel while Kat lies unconscious in the back, breathing and groaning.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. BMW, TALLINN STREET – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist tells Neil to pull alongside the backwards Audi, opens his door, fumbles to open the Audi door while calling to Kat, sees her swing her bound legs around in the back, but his door hits the Audi door and slams it closed again as a logjam of traffic looms closer.

scenes.heading: EXT. DOCKSIDE AREA, EDGE OF TALLINN – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist is pulled from the trunk and marched toward an entrance, seeing Sator in a respirator walking backwards and pulling Kat forwards toward a different entrance through a steel fence, and he passes shipping containers marked Oslo Freeport.

scenes.heading: INT. STORAGE FACILITY, TALLINN FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist is taken past the table of weapons, through a large door, and through red doors with a porthole.

scenes.heading: INT. TURNSTILE, TALLINN FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist is pushed into a chair in a red-lit chamber divided by glass, where on the blue-lit other side Sator enters backwards pulling Kat in a respirator, shoves her into a chair, and places his gun against her head while playing recordings in reverse to interrogate the Protagonist, who eventually reveals the algorithm is in the BMW's glove box after Sator threatens to shoot Kat.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist breaks under torture threat to Kat and reveals the algorithm's location, giving Sator what he needs — a devastating reversal after the successful heist.

scenes.heading: INT. TURNSTILE, TALLINN FREEPORT – MOMENTS EARLIER
scenes.summary: Sator runs out of the turnstile into the blue side and watches his older self interrogate the Protagonist through the glass, then fires up his phone and steps up to the glass as the Protagonist shouts that it's in the glove box, and Sator counts down while Kat screams, then grabs her and pushes her up against the glass.

scenes.heading: INT. TURNSTILE, TALLINN FREEPORT – THE PRESENT
scenes.summary: The Protagonist looks through the glass at Kat as paramilitaries pull her out saying she's been shot, Neil enters with Ives, the Protagonist grabs Neil and throws him against the wall demanding to know if he talked, and Ives explains that nobody talked but that Sator's team is running a temporal pincer movement where inverted soldiers relay intelligence back through time.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist learns the devastating truth about temporal warfare — Sator knew their every move because his inverted team experienced the ambush and reported back through time.

scenes.heading: EXT. YARD, TALLINN FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist emerges into the inverted world where birds fly backwards and steam contracts, spots the Saab in the yard, runs over, and finds the keys sitting on the front tire.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SAAB – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist searches the Saab's glove box, wheel well, and back seat but finds nothing, then gives up, starts the engine, and pulls out gently to feel out the strange handling.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SAAB, TALLINN STREETS – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist steers the car around backwards-moving traffic and pedestrians while marveling at the inverted world, notices people stare as if they already know a backwards-driven car is heading their way, checks the phone showing a blinking dot half a mile away, and guns it.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The firefight from before plays in reverse with Neil laying down fire and Kat carried back to the Audi, while Sator moving forwards checks the BMW's glove compartment, finds nothing, and fuming heads back to the Mercedes ducking behind cars as bullets fly.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. SAAB – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist pulls onto a highway where cars face him but move in his direction, races along as the only normal-looking car among cars speeding backwards, spots the empty orange case by the roadside, pulls over, jumps out, and wedges his Bluetooth earpiece between the foam padding and plastic to bug it.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY, TALLINN – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Mercedes races back up the off-ramp with the Saab following through backwards traffic, and the Protagonist's phone picks up audio fragments from his Bluetooth bug revealing Sator saying the material's not in the case and ordering other sections of the algorithm taken to the hypocentre.

scenes.heading: INT. /EXT. SAAB – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist tracks the Mercedes as Sator climbs from it into the Audi with the orange case, the Protagonist lowers his window, hears banging as the black metal shape unwedges from his seat and bounces around, the Saab cuts between the Audi and BMW, and the orange case flies through the Protagonist's window while the algorithm flies out into Sator's.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist successfully retrieves the algorithm from Sator by exploiting inverted physics — the objects swap between vehicles, reversing Sator's victory.

scenes.heading: INT. SHIPPING CONTAINER – LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist wakes on a stretcher next to Kat with Neil sitting between them, learns he may have the first case of hypothermia from a gasoline explosion due to reversed heat transfer, discovers they're in a Rotas shipping container heading back to Oslo, admits he handed Sator the material, argues with Neil about trust and half-truths, and when Kat wakes she reveals Sator is dying and wants to take the world with him.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARGO SHIP – LATER
scenes.summary: The cargo ship steams majestically backwards through the cold North Sea with water unsplashing at the bow and sucking underneath the stern.

scenes.heading: INT. SHIPPING CONTAINER – LATER
scenes.summary: Banging wakes the Protagonist and Neil reports from a peephole that they're on a truck and shouldn't be too long now.

scenes.heading: INT. SHIPPING CONTAINER – LATER
scenes.summary: Neil notices the Protagonist examining a lesion on his arm from getting nicked in Tallinn, and they discuss the grandfather paradox — whether destroying their ancestors would destroy the future people — with Neil explaining there's no answer because it's a paradox and suggesting the future believes the past is resilient.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME – LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist peers through the peephole and sees the wrecked plane outside the collapsed wall sealed with steel fencing, realizes they weren't taken inside, tells Neil they'll use the breach and the chaos right after impact, and they both look at the unconscious Kat wondering if she's healed enough.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME – NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Protagonist watches through the peephole as cleanup crews swarm backwards, sirens start, water flows up onto the plane's wings, fire crews unpack their equipment, and he winces pulling his injured arm into its sleeve while explaining the plan to Neil and noticing a hole in his suit directly over the lesion.

scenes.heading: EXT. APRON, OSLO AIRPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist hurries backwards pulling the gurney with Kat through pandemonium as fire crews battle the blaze, water streams rise onto the plane, a sheet of flaming metal spins off the ground nearly hitting them, and he spots a line under the fuselage toward the breach.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FREEPORT SHIPPING AREA – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist draws his gun, tells Neil to wait, rushes up onto the loading dock, hears backwards voices on the other side of a roller door, and is smashed through the bottom of the door when the loose jet engine turns and blasts inverted thrust at him.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULT, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist crashes into himself and Neil dressed as businessmen from their earlier visit, tackles his earlier self who throws him down and gets his gun, shouts incomprehensibly while kicking his injured arm repeatedly making him scream, puts the gun to his head until Earlier Neil grabs his arm, and they struggle rolling across the floor with the gun between them until it fires.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULT CORRIDOR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist runs pursued by Earlier Neil who grabs his gas mask and yanks it off, sees the unmasked Protagonist's face, looks surprised, and bolts back the way he came while the Protagonist watches him go then turns.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULT CORRIDOR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist races down the angled corridor and skids through the doorway into the smoke-filled cargo area where he left Neil and Kat.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULT 2, FREEPORT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil races up to the Earlier Protagonist and grabs his arm telling him not to kill the masked man.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FREEPORT SHIPPING AREA – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist emerges to find Neil in a respirator guarding Kat strapped to the gurney with the fire crew battling the blazing plane behind them, points right, ducks through the breach into the chaos, and Neil moves backwards toward the outside.

scenes.heading: EXT. BREACH – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist runs through smoke and flame dodging debris falling from the plane toward the emergency services, creeps along the side of an ambulance, and when the driver spots him in the mirror he darts in and hits the driver in the face.

scenes.heading: INT. VAULT CORRIDOR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil moving forwards ducks behind the corner as Earlier Protagonist and Earlier Neil in business suits walk backwards away from the Rotas vault, then pushes Kat toward the broken roller door and inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. APRON, OSLO AIRPORT – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist swerves the ambulance trying to get close to the breach, spins it around as close as possible, jumps out, opens the back, peers into the smoke and water waiting, and Neil emerges pushing the gurney with Kat.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. AMBULANCE – MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist drives the ambulance with sirens blaring, asks Neil why he didn't say he knew it was the Protagonist coming out of the vault, and Neil explains he couldn't risk changing what happened and reveals their policy is to suppress such information because they're the people saving the world from what might have been.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PARKED AMBULANCE – NIGHT
scenes.summary: Neil and the Protagonist check on Kat in the parked ambulance as her eyes open, Neil confirms she'll be okay with a scar, Kat protests that Sator has the algorithm and they don't know where or when he is, and the Protagonist tells Neil to get Priya to Oslo because if he doesn't do something now she'll tell him about the 241 in two days.

scenes.heading: EXT. OSLO WAR MEMORIAL – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist follows Priya through a crowd at a war memorial, takes her by the arm, confronts her about nearly getting Kat killed, reveals he let Sator get the algorithm, demands to know about it, and Priya explains it was built by a scientist in the future who killed herself so she couldn't be forced to make another and that the algorithm can invert the entropy of the world which would be the end of everything.

scenes.heading: EXT. WIND FARM, NORWAY – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. ICEBREAKER, BARENTS SEA – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. ICEBREAKER – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. HOLD, ICEBREAKER – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. CABIN, ICEBREAKER – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. ICEBREAKER, BARENTS SEA – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. TURNSTILE, ICEBREAKER – DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. AIRLOCK – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist tells Kat that Neil has already gone through the airlock and explains she must travel back another day to reach Vietnam and get back on the yacht, where someone will help her save her child.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CHINOOK – LATER
scenes.summary: The Protagonist watches the icebreaker recede from the Chinook.

scenes.heading: EXT. VAST BARREN PLAIN, DUSTED WITH SNOW – DAY
scenes.summary: Three Chinooks fly low across a barren snowy plain, followed by three more long-lining shipping containers.

scenes.heading: INT. CHINOOK – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist sits among a dozen soldiers in the Chinook watching the northern plain pass beneath them.

scenes.heading: EXT. TENET CAMP – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist disembarks from the Chinook at Tenet camp and follows others to a tent as another Chinook sets down a container.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIEFING TENT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ives briefs Red team on the temporal pincer movement at Stalsk-12, explaining they will clear the landing zone for Blue team's evacuation while Blue team moves forward through the same ten minutes inverted.

scenes.heading: EXT. COAST OF VIET NAM – DAY
scenes.summary: Mahir brings Kat by speedboat to Sator's yacht off the coast of Vietnam, where they watch her earlier self and her son board a tender and head for shore.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIEFING TENT – DAY
scenes.summary: Ives explains to Red team that the radioactive area is defended with both inverse and conventional ordinance and bi-temporal antagonists, while Neil (in an insert cut) takes a blue armband with Blue team.

scenes.heading: EXT. COAST OF VIET NAM – DAY
scenes.summary: Mahir shows Kat how to use a pistol, confirms she can dive forty feet from the private deck, and tells her not to let Sator die until she sees his flare signal as the chopper lifts off.

scenes.heading: EXT. TENET CAMP – DAY
scenes.summary: Soldiers head for the Chinooks, some staring at the containers as the helicopters lift off carrying them.

scenes.heading: EXT. COAST OF VIET NAM – DAY
scenes.summary: Mahir pulls up fifty yards from Sator's yacht and Kat slips into the water with the pistol bag.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. NORTHERN PLAIN – DAY
scenes.summary: The Chinooks fly low over the plain toward Stalsk-12, a set of concrete structures fused to terraced strip-mined land.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATOR’S YACHT, COAST OF VIET NAM – DAY
scenes.summary: Kat quietly pulls herself onto a rear platform of Sator's yacht and stealthily makes her way up through the boat.

scenes.heading: EXT. LZ, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist leaps from the hovering Chinook as anti-aircraft guns start firing at the helicopters approaching the landing zone.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Chinooks long-lining containers fly backwards toward a giant depression at the hypocentre ridge.

scenes.heading: INT. CONTAINER – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil looks out a peephole as Wheeler warns they're coming in on the shock wave and the container shudders violently.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The shock wave contracts as the Chinook surfs backwards toward the hypocentre with energy building and ground lifting and flattening.

scenes.heading: EXT. LZ, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Red team charge and take out antiaircraft emplacements, then draw the enemy back into the city as Blue team scrambles backwards out of the containers behind them.
scenes.turningPointNote: The temporal pincer movement begins with both teams engaging simultaneously in opposite temporal directions

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Blue team runs forward out of the containers led by Neil, blasting at enemy soldiers on the ridge as a grey truck reverses toward the hole with a rope taut into it, and Neil shouts to Wheeler about the capsule.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kat finds a junior crew member cleaning up smashed raspberries, tells him she snuck back to surprise Andrei, and asks him to have Sator find her on the private deck without telling the others.

scenes.heading: EXT. LZ, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Red team heads toward the turnstile building as Wheeler runs backwards toward a bunker that unexplodes, then they hit the bunker with an RPG and race around the building.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Wheeler leads Blue team down the hill chasing backwards-running enemy as landmines explode and implode, and Neil dives out of the way as the grey truck races backwards through the minefield triggering mines.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Kat slides the pistol bag under the daybed, unclips the bottom safety cable at the rail, and looks at Mahir's boat across the water.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist jumps away as concrete chunks vibrate and fly violently up into a building gap as an inverse RPG zips back into a window, which Ives then blasts with a forward RPG.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil checks his watch and heads downhill toward the city where Blue team beats back the enemy.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Kat hoses raspberries and glass from the deck and turns the water on herself as Sator appears, and she tells him they need time together and moves toward him as he calls to have the tender brought back.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist spots the tunnel entrance and calls Ives over to form a splinter unit as Ives races across under fire.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12
scenes.summary: Neil sees Wheeler and a Blue soldier take shelter in a breach as dust gathers around it, shouts a warning, and watches massive concrete chunks fly overhead and reassemble on top of the Blue soldier, crushing him as Wheeler dives clear.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sator tells Kat he'll get Max brought back to share the sunset moment with them as Kat looks uneasily at the shore.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist says they're running out of time but Ives warns they need a distraction, then looks ahead at a leaning ruin of a building top.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil is pinned down and frustrated as Wheeler shelters with Blue team and prepares an RPG soldier to take out the base of a building on her mark.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Ives watch debris roll and gather momentum toward the leaning ruin as Ives signals the RPG soldier and fires on his mark, causing the ruin to thrust upwards.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Wheeler watches as the leaning ruin's upper storeys reassemble while its base explodes.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Ives race at the tunnel entrance as the grey truck races at them and they dive for the tunnel while the ruin's base reassembles and upper storeys explode.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Wheeler ducks as the building collapses and Neil lifts his head after the explosion/implosion, then gets up and starts running.

scenes.heading: EXT. TUNNEL ENTRANCE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Ives race into the tunnel and hit a tripwire that blows the tunnel entrance down behind them as they jump inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sator holds a silver pill in the sunlight and tells Kat it's the way the world ends, borrowed from the CIA, and when he takes it everything is over, but Kat seductively asks him to wait for the sunset and vodka.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAHIR’S BOAT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir checks his radio and hears Ives report that the situation is not clear.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Ives look back at the collapsed entrance, acknowledge they're committed, and race through the mining tunnel as Ives radios to hold tight.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil spots Sator's grey MI8 overhead and sees Volkov reverse-exit the tunnel mouth, derig a tripwired booby trap, and leap onto a line rising from the streets, then Neil takes off sprinting as Wheeler watches him go.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Ives run down the tunnel and find a body with a blue armband lying beyond bars at the end, with the capsule visible in the hypocentre cavern, and the Protagonist freezes when he finds a coin talisman attached to the body's zip.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist discovers the body with his coin talisman, realizing it's Neil who has died to save the mission

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Sator speaks quietly into the phone about his fate being bound up with radiation and making a devil's bargain of money for time, as Kat looks at Mahir's boat and sees the tender approaching in the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist looks at Volkov and the bars between him and the capsule as Sator tells him over the phone that when he dies the world dies with him and the Protagonist's knowledge dies buried like an anonymous Egyptian builder.

scenes.heading: INT. TURNSTILE BUILDING, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil enters the turnstile room, watches enemy troops invert through the machine, waits for the room to empty, sees himself through the proving window, and runs at the machine.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – EVENING
scenes.summary: Sator tells the Protagonist over the phone that he's creating a new world where entropy will invert like magnetic poles switching, and time itself will switch direction as the same sunshine will warm the same people going the other way.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist asks how the future can want to destroy them and Sator explains over the phone that their world shriveled because of us, their oceans rose and rivers ran dry, so they have no choice but to turn back.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sator tells the Protagonist over the phone that the future is looking out for its own survival, and when the Protagonist calls him a traitor bringing death because he has no life left, Sator says life continues and his greatest sin was bringing a son into a world he knew was ending.

scenes.heading: EXT. CITY STREETS, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil, now moving forwards, jumps in the grey truck outside the turnstile building and races through the streets past the imploding/exploding building toward the tunnel mouth, trying to head off the Protagonist and Ives, but they dive in and the tunnel collapses, so Neil guns the engine toward the hypocentre ridge.

scenes.heading: EXT. LZ, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Wheeler leads Blue team with their wounded toward the lowering Chinooks and containers as they see Red team reassembling the anti-aircraft guns.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist watches Volkov pick up the algorithm and tells Sator over the phone that without belief he's not human but a madman or god, and Volkov slowly backs toward the capsule with the algorithm as the Protagonist looks down and sees Ives's hand moving.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sator tells the Protagonist over the phone that Kat will die after they meet, then says he'll give her his love instead.

scenes.heading: INT. HYPOCENTRE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Volkov seals the capsule and prepares to drop it into the deep bore hole while the Protagonist watches Ives's hand emerge with a sidearm.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – SUNSET
scenes.summary: Sator pauses before hanging up and calls out to Volkov.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Volkov rushes forward with his gun following Sator's order to shoot, but the dead Tenet soldier absorbs the bullet and leaps to one side, alive.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sator hears the gunshot and appears satisfied while Kat pulls the phone away and tells him enough business.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist grabs Ives's gun and shoots Volkov before he can release the winch, while Ives sees the lock is open.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil races up the slope through the minefield, setting off personnel mines in his wake.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – EVENING
scenes.summary: Sator tells Kat the sun will rise where he commands it, she sprays down the deck claiming she spilled sunscreen, then he asks her to rub sunscreen on his back.

scenes.heading: INT. TUNNEL, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Ives race through the gate just before it slams shut, then the Protagonist struggles with the capsule hanging over the well while Ives radios Mahir.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAHIR’S BOAT – EVENING
scenes.summary: Mahir looks through binoculars with a flare gun ready and receives Ives's order to hold.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Neil reverses toward the hole, jumps out, and pulls a line from the rear winch.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – SUNSET
scenes.summary: Kat sees her earlier self approaching with Max, jumps up from the daybed, tells Sator she can't let him think he's won, and pulls a gun from the diver's bag.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kat decides to kill Sator, triggering the climactic sequence and forcing the team to extract the algorithm before the explosion.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAHIR’S BOAT – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Mahir sees Sator's body pitch into the sea and radios that Kat has killed him, then guns the engine toward the yacht.

scenes.heading: INT. HYPOCENTRE, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: The Protagonist opens the capsule revealing the algorithm while Ives tells him Kat killed Sator early, then a line drops between them.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Neil watches soldiers lift off, checks his watch counting down to zero, then guns the truck and pulls away from the hole.

scenes.heading: INT. HYPOCENTRE, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist and Ives are yanked into the air clutching the algorithm and bounce painfully over the lip of the hole.

scenes.heading: EXT. HYPOCENTRE RIDGE, STALSK-12 – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The truck drags them away as the ground explodes and collapses, the line snaps, and Ives pulls the Protagonist back onto solid ground as the subsidence approaches.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist and Ives narrowly escape with the algorithm as the hypocentre explodes, completing the mission's objective.

scenes.heading: EXT. LZ, STALSK-12 – DAY
scenes.summary: Wheeler pulls the last Blue team soldier into the container as her watch hits zero and the door slams shut.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE DECK, SATOR’S YACHT – SUNSET
scenes.summary: Kat picks up the shell casing, washes away blood and sunscreen, dives into the water, and her earlier self catches a glimpse of her diving.

scenes.heading: EXT. COAST OF VIET NAM – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kat tells the Protagonist over radio she couldn't let Sator die thinking he'd won, the Protagonist confirms they found a way, and Mahir's boat speeds away dragging Sator's body.

scenes.heading: EXT. WINDSWEPT PLAIN OUTSIDE STALSK-12 – EVENING
scenes.summary: Ives picks up the algorithm and suggests they each take a piece and hide it, the Protagonist agrees, and Neil volunteers to hide his piece.
scenes.turningPointNote: The team decides to split the algorithm into three pieces and hide them separately, establishing the post-mission security protocol.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL GATES – AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Kat walks toward the school gates, notices a car with tinted windows shadowing her, and gets out the dumb phone the Protagonist gave her.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CAR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist enters Priya's car with a gun, confronts her about trying to kill Kat, and plays a voicemail recording from Kat.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCHOOL GATES – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kat hits send and speaks into the phone reporting her location and saying it's probably nothing.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CAR – CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Protagonist tells Priya he's the protagonist and has been in charge all along, shoots her through the seat back, and watches Kat greet Max as she tosses the phone away.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Protagonist kills Priya and reveals he's been the true leader of the operation, protecting Kat and completing his final loose end.
